qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: VerifyHostToken accepts forged JWT tokens enabling host endpoint authentication bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding reports acceptance of forged or expired host JWTs because VerifyHostToken omits token.Valid, while the advisory describes valid host tokens bypassing per-resource authorization checks in host-allowed routes.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing HTTP server timeouts enable Slowloris denial-of-service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Unrelated root cause: this finding concerns missing HTTP server timeouts causing Slowloris denial of service, not host-token authorization checks in Netmaker's Authorise middleware.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described flaw: valid host tokens accepted on hostAllowed routes without verifying that the host is authorized for the requested node, host, or related resource.
evidence: GHSA-hmqr-wjmj-376c describes insufficient authorization in Netmaker's Authorise middleware: a valid host token can access, modify, or delete resources belonging to other hosts when object identifiers are known.

jwt_host_token_bypass/vulnerability_report.json identifies a different authentication flaw in logic/jwts.go where VerifyHostToken accepts invalidly signed or expired JWTs because it checks token != nil instead of token.Valid.
slowloris_timeout_dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies missing HTTP server timeout configuration in controllers/controller.go, unrelated to host-token authorization.
